今日最新

'World-first' Vaccine Designed by Artificial Intelligence

来源： BBC News | 日期： 2026年6月6日
🔗 原文出处链接

文章摘要

剑桥大学研究团队首次利用人工智能设计出一种"超级抗原"疫苗，可对抗多种冠状病毒及潜在的新型病毒。该疫苗已在人体试验中证明安全有效，标志着疫苗研发方式的根本性变革，为预防未来疫情提供了新思路。

英文正文

Artificial intelligence has been used to develop a "fundamentally new" type of vaccine that could protect against large swathes of viruses and prevent pandemics, say researchers.

The team at the University of Cambridge say it is the first time a vaccine's key component has been designed entirely by AI and then trialled in people. The vaccine was engineered to work on all coronaviruses, which would include all Covid variants as well as viruses that currently infect animals yet have the potential to start the next pandemic.

Vaccines teach our bodies how to spot an infection to increase our chances of fighting it off. But some viruses are adept at changing their appearance – or mutating – so vaccines can quickly go out of date. It's why Covid and winter flu vaccines need to be regularly updated.

"We're always behind," said Prof Jonathan Heeney, from the University of Cambridge. "What we're trying to do is get ahead of the curve and so far ahead they could protect against new outbreaks or pandemics."

Normally vaccines are designed using a current strain of a virus. The Cambridge researchers took known genetic codes from a range of coronaviruses that had been recorded by surveillance programmes hunting for potential viral threats. These genetic codes were analysed by an artificial intelligence. It then designed a "super-antigen" that could train the immune system in such a way it gave protection against the whole family of viruses – even if they mutated or a new infection jumped from animals to people.

Antigens are the critical components of vaccines as this is what the immune system learns to attack. Heeney said this was the first time an antigen designed by AI had been trialled in people. He said the technology was "surprising all of us" and it was "amazing what we can do with it for the good of humanity."

"This is about making vaccines that protect us, not just from today's viruses, but protect us from what can cause the next outbreak or disease," Heeney told BBC News. "This is a fundamental shift in how we prepare for pandemics."

The trials, in 39 people, were designed to assess if such vaccines were safe. A second study – involving around 200 people – will give a greater understanding of how well it is training the immune system. Prof Saul Faust, who performed some of the trials at the University of Southampton, said the AI design "definitely has potential" and was "really exciting."

Prof Andy Pollard, the director of the Oxford Vaccine Group, said artificial intelligence was going to be a "game changer" for vaccine research. He added that AI tools had the potential to predict how the immune system would respond to a vaccine, making development much faster and would "save lives."

Science Minister Lord Vallance said: "Another British science success story, this is a great example of how we can bring our research expertise together with AI to deliver new treatments. With the first human trials showing positive results, this work could help speed up the roll out vaccines to benefit people all over the world for the long-term."

重点词汇表

词汇 音标/词性 中文释义 等级 例句
artificial intelligence /ˌɑːtɪfɪʃl ɪnˈtelɪdʒəns/ n. 人工智能 ★★★★★ Artificial intelligence is transforming many industries.
vaccine /ˈvæksiːn/ n. 疫苗 ★★★★★ The vaccine provides protection against the disease.
pandemic /pænˈdemɪk/ n. 全球性流行病 ★★★★★ The COVID-19 pandemic affected the entire world.
mutate /mjuːˈteɪt/ v. 变异，突变 ★★★★ Viruses can mutate and become more dangerous.
immune system /ɪˈmjuːn ˈsɪstəm/ n. 免疫系统 ★★★★★ A strong immune system helps fight infections.
antigen /ˈæntɪdʒən/ n. 抗原 ★★ Antigens trigger the immune response.
trial /ˈtraɪəl/ n./v. 试验，审判 ★★★★ The new drug is currently in clinical trials.
strain /streɪn/ n. 菌株，品种；压力 ★★★★ Scientists identified a new strain of the virus.
surveillance /səˈveɪləns/ n. 监控，监视 ★★★ Health surveillance programs monitor disease outbreaks.
potential /pəˈtenʃl/ n./adj. 潜力，潜在的 ★★★★★ The technology has great potential for medical use.
component /kəmˈpəʊnənt/ n. 组成部分，成分 ★★★★ Each component plays a vital role in the system.
fundamental /ˌfʌndəˈmentl/ adj. 根本的，基础的 ★★★★★ This represents a fundamental change in approach.
protect against /prəˈtekt əˈɡenst/ phr. 抵御，防止 ★★★★★ Vaccines protect against various diseases.
game changer /geɪm ˈtʃeɪndʒə/ n. 颠覆性创新，改变格局的事物 ★★★ AI is a game changer in vaccine development.
roll out /rəʊl aʊt/ phr. 推出，推广 ★★★★ The government plans to roll out the vaccine nationwide.

长难句解析

"The vaccine was engineered to work on all coronaviruses, which would include all Covid variants as well as viruses that currently infect animals yet have the potential to start the next pandemic."

定语从句 并列结构

结构分析：主句为"The vaccine was engineered to work on all coronaviruses"。which引导非限制性定语从句，修饰先行词coronaviruses。定语从句中包含as well as连接的并列成分，以及that引导的定语从句修饰viruses。

翻译：该疫苗被设计用于对抗所有冠状病毒，包括所有新冠变异株以及目前感染动物但有可能引发下一次大流行的病毒。

"It then designed a 'super-antigen' that could train the immune system in such a way it gave protection against the whole family of viruses – even if they mutated or a new infection jumped from animals to people."

定语从句 状语从句 such...that结构

结构分析：that引导定语从句修饰super-antigen。in such a way后接结果状语（省略that）。even if引导让步状语从句，表示即使发生变异或跨物种传播，疫苗仍能提供保护。

翻译：人工智能随后设计出一种"超级抗原"，能够训练免疫系统以提供对整个病毒家族的保护——即使病毒发生变异或新感染从动物传播到人类。

"He added that AI tools had the potential to predict how the immune system would respond to a vaccine, making development much faster and would 'save lives.'

宾语从句 非谓语动词 嵌套结构

结构分析：that引导宾语从句作added的宾语。宾语从句中how引导宾语从句作predict的宾语。making development much faster是现在分词作结果状语，表示AI预测能力带来的结果。

翻译：他补充说，AI工具有潜力预测免疫系统对疫苗的反应，从而加快研发进程并"拯救生命"。

"With the first human trials showing positive results, this work could help speed up the roll out vaccines to benefit people all over the world for the long-term."

with复合结构 非谓语动词

结构分析：With the first human trials showing positive results是with复合结构作状语，表示背景条件。showing是现在分词修饰trials。speed up后接宾语roll out，benefit是不定式作目的状语。

翻译：随着首次人体试验显示积极结果，这项工作有助于加快疫苗推广，长期造福全球人民。

高考写作素材积累

1. "fundamentally new" — 根本性的创新

用法：用于描述突破性的技术或方法。

例句：AI represents a fundamentally new approach to problem-solving.

科技发展

2. "get ahead of the curve" — 走在前列，提前应对

用法：用于描述前瞻性思维和预防措施。

例句：We need to get ahead of the curve in environmental protection.

环境保护 青年责任

3. "game changer" — 颠覆性创新

用法：用于描述改变行业格局的技术或事件。

例句：Renewable energy is a game changer for sustainable development.

科技发展 环境保护

4. "protect against" — 抵御，防止

用法：用于描述防护措施，适用于健康、环境等话题。

例句：We should protect against potential risks through education.

健康生活 环境保护

5. "speed up the roll out" — 加快推广

用法：用于描述政策或技术的实施推广。

例句：The government plans to speed up the roll out of green technology.

科技发展 社会发展

6. "benefit people all over the world" — 造福全球人民

用法：用于描述国际合作和全球议题。

例句：Scientific cooperation can benefit people all over the world.

国际视野 社会公益

7. "for the good of humanity" — 为了人类福祉

用法：用于描述科技伦理和社会责任。

例句：Technology should be developed for the good of humanity.

青年责任 国际视野

讨论问题

Q1: What are the advantages of using AI in vaccine development compared to traditional methods?

问题翻译：与传统方法相比，使用人工智能进行疫苗研发有哪些优势？

答题提示：

可以从以下角度回答：①速度更快（speed up development）；②能够预测病毒变异（predict mutations）；③设计出更广泛的保护（broader protection）；④节省资源、降低成本（save resources and costs）。注意使用文中词汇如"game changer"、"get ahead of the curve"等。

Q2: Why is it important to develop vaccines that can protect against multiple viruses?

问题翻译：为什么开发能够抵御多种病毒的疫苗很重要？

答题提示：

可以从以下角度回答：①病毒容易变异（viruses mutate easily）；②传统疫苗需要定期更新（need regular updates）；③预防未来疫情（prevent future pandemics）；④保护全球健康（protect global health）。可引用文中"We're always behind"和"get ahead of the curve"的观点。

Q3: How might AI technology change the future of medical research and healthcare?

问题翻译：人工智能技术可能如何改变医学研究和医疗保健的未来？

答题提示：

可以从以下角度拓展：①加速药物研发（accelerate drug development）；②个性化医疗（personalized medicine）；③疾病预测和预防（disease prediction and prevention）；④提高医疗效率（improve healthcare efficiency）。可结合高考作文话题"科技发展"、"健康生活"展开论述，使用"game changer"、"fundamental shift"等表达。

本文仅供高三学生英语学习使用 | 来源：BBC News

生成日期：2026年6月24日
